ASSISTANTMESSAGE #7
Good, I can see the debug page. Let me analyze what I see: 1. **Objects (10)**: Stove, Fridge, Tea Set, Kotatsu, TV, Bookshelf, Desk, Bed, Easel, Bonsai - Most show idle state - Stove: 1/1 free (capacity 1) - Tea Set: full (capacity 1, currently all slots occupied) - Kotatsu: 2/2 free (capacity 2) - TV: 2/2 free (capacity 2) - Bookshelf, Desk, Bed, Easel, and Bonsai each have 1/1 free capacity - Fridge doesn't show slot information 2. **Characters**: - Maple and Sol are both idle at their respective positions - Nyx is walking toward the tea set , which is reserved 3. **Event Log**: - All three characters decided to interact with the tea set at 8:01 AM, each wanting to brew tea - The tea set is at full capacity (1/1), so only one character can use it This creates a conflict—Nyx is already heading to the tea set while Maple and Sol also want to use it. Since the tea set can only accommodate one character at a time, I need to see how the game resolves this queue situation. The AI timer shows 26 seconds until the next decision cycle, so I should wait and observe whether Maple and Sol find alternative options or remain idle while Nyx uses the tea set.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #10
Interesting! Now at 8:19 AM, I can see: - **Tea Set**: brewing, full (Nyx is using it), 1s remaining - **Bookshelf**: reading, full (Maple is using it), 34s remaining - **Sol**: idle with no target Looking at the event log, I can see: - 8:13 AM: bookshelf idle → reading (auto) - 8:05 AM: tea_set idle → brewing (auto) And the initial decisions at 8:00-8:01 were: - Maple: interact → tea set - Sol: interact → tea set - Nyx: interact → tea set So all three agents targeted the tea set, but with capacity 1, only Nyx got it first at 8:00 AM. Maple must have fallen back to the bookshelf when the tea set was unavailable, and Sol ended up idle since both the tea set and bookshelf were taken. Looking at the event log, I see the tea set started brewing at 8:05 AM and the bookshelf started reading at 8:13 AM, but I don't see explicit FALLBACK events recorded. I need to check if there's more event data to understand whether the fallback behavior is being logged separately or if it's happening silently when agents can't access their preferred target. I'm confirming the AI timer shows 11 seconds remaining, and I notice Nyx claimed the tea set while Maple settled at the bookshelf instead. Let me speed this up to 4x to watch more decision cycles unfold.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #14
Good, now I'm at 4x speed (8:49 AM). Let me observe more. Key findings from this snapshot: - Event log now shows more events - At 8:30 AM, both Sol and Nyx made new decisions: - Sol: interact → tea set (when the first one was done) - Nyx: interact → easel - Tea set state transitions: idle → brewing (8:05 AM) → ready (8:20 AM) → idle (8:31 AM) But I notice something important: - At 8:01 AM, all THREE characters (Maple, Sol, Nyx) decided to use the tea set (capacity 1) - Only one could actually use it at a time, yet I'm not seeing any fallback events logged when the others were rejected Looking at the current state: - Maple: interacting with bookshelf (Hold: 4s) - Sol: walking with no target shown, which seems odd - Nyx: interacting with easel (Hold: 27s, at position (2,6)) At 8:30 AM, Sol decided to interact with the tea set again, but the lack of fallback events in the log is concerning. The state machines look correct—tea set cycles through idle → brewing → ready → idle, and the easel goes idle → painting. But something's off with Sol's behavior: the tea set transitioned from ready to idle right after Sol initiated interaction, yet Sol is now walking with no target. That interaction completed suspiciously fast, and I need to understand why Sol ended up walking nowhere instead of moving to the tea set or staying put. Let me trace through the timeline more carefully. Nyx started using the tea set around 8:00 AM with "1s remaining" shown in that first snapshot, which means the interaction finished almost immediately at 4x speed. Then Sol tried to interact with the tea set at 8:30 AM, and by 8:31 AM the tea set was idle again—so Sol's use also completed in about a minute of game time. The question is whether Sol actually reached the tea set or if something interrupted the interaction, leaving Sol in this walking state with no destination. Looking back at the earlier snapshot, Nyx was wrapping up with the tea set around 8:19 AM, which explains why it transitioned to ready by 8:20 AM and became available for Sol's second interaction at 8:30 AM. As for Maple, they started using the bookshelf sometime between 8:04 AM and 8:19 AM, coinciding with that idle → reading state change at 8:13 AM. The puzzle is that the event log doesn't show a FALLBACK event for Maple switching from the tea set to the bookshelf, even though the timestamps suggest Maple made that transition around 8:13 AM. Either the log is incomplete and missing some entries, or Maple's decision to use the bookshelf happened during an AI cycle that wasn't fully captured in what's visible here. I'm noticing the log distinguishes between AI decision events and automatic state transitions, but Maple's decision to visit the bookshelf isn't showing up as an event—it might be a fallback action that's logged differently, or the event log could be incomplete or truncated. handled silently Actually, looking at the bookshelf event: "8:13 AM: bookshelf idle → reading" (auto) - this tells us the bookshelf started being used at 8:13 AM. But there's no corresponding character AI decision in the log for this. This suggests Maple's bookshelf interaction was either a FALLBACK (shown differently) or the AI decision event was scrolled off or not logged. Actually, looking at the event log area from the snapshots - the event log only shows 10 entries at a time, and they seem to be the most recent ones. The log might have been filtering out some events that happened between 8:01 AM and 8:05 AM. Hmm, but if all three characters decided to go to tea set at 8:01 AM (capacity 1), then Nyx got it (first decision at 8:00 AM), and Maple and Sol should have been blocked or fallen back. Since Maple ended up at the bookshelf at 8:13 AM, there must have been a fallback decision that isn't visible in the current log view. The event log seems to have a 10-entry limit, so earlier decisions between 8:01 and 8:05 AM are likely hidden. Looking at the character positions from the initial screenshot, I need to check where Maple and Sol actually ended up to understand what happened during that gap. position (10,6) - Nyx: walking → tea set (reserved) So at the start (8:04 AM), Maple and Sol were already idle. They decided to use tea set at 8:01 AM but both had no target. This suggests both were blocked/couldn't use the tea set (Nyx reserved it). Then by 8:13 AM, Maple went to bookshelf. Between 8:01 AM and 8:13 AM, what happened? The event log doesn't show any Maple or Sol decisions. This means either: 1. Their decisions were not logged (FALLBACK or idle decisions?) 2. The event log only has limited space and older events scrolled off Let me reset and observe from the beginning with more careful attention. Actually, let me keep watching the 4x speed run for now and collect more data points. I'll grab another screenshot in a moment to see what's changed.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #18
Now I have a much richer view at 11:41 AM game time. Let me analyze what I see: **Object States:** - Stove: idle, 1/1 free - Fridge: idle, 1/1 free (but it shows "full" pip? Actually it shows a filled pip which is confusing - let me re-read) - Tea Set: idle, "full" shown with filled pip - but the state says idle... wait, looking at the icon: - Tea Set: state "idle", slot shows "● full" (filled orange/yellow pip) -- Wait, is this a color difference? The pip is orange/yellow, which might mean something different... Looking more carefully at the bookshelf, I see it's actively in use with "39s remaining" and a filled pip, which makes sense for an ongoing interaction. For the Tea Set, the "full" indicator with a filled pip while the state shows "idle" suggests the tea is ready and prepared, but nobody's currently using it—so "full" describes the item's readiness state rather than an active interaction. Looking at the current snapshot data more carefully, the Tea Set is showing idle state with a full pip indicator, while the Fridge displays idle with an available slot. I need to verify what these pip states actually represent in the current moment rather than relying on older snapshots. So the pattern is becoming clearer - when a slot shows "full" but the state is "idle", it means a character has reserved it but hasn't arrived yet. Sol is walking toward the Bonsai with it reserved, which explains that discrepancy. For the Tea Set, I'm trying to figure out who reserved it since none of the visible characters seem to be targeting it, which suggests either there's another character I'm missing or something else is going on with how the reservation system works. Let me pull a fresh snapshot of the event log to see what's actually happening right now - the screenshot is a bit dated, so I need the most recent interactions to understand the current state properly. Looking at the full timeline, I can see the pattern of activities across the morning: Sol and Nyx have been moving between different objects and locations, with the bonsai, tea set, fridge, and easel all cycling through various states. The interactions show a mix of automated transitions and character-initiated actions with their own thoughts about what they're doing. I'm noticing something interesting—at 11:22 AM, Nyx moved to the kotatsu without interacting with an object, which suggests there's a "wander" action separate from object use. I need to dig deeper into the event log to find fallback behavior, talk interactions between characters, and what happens when multiple characters want the same limited-capacity object. ) while the animation hasn't started yet. So "idle" describes the object state and "full" describes the slot occupancy—they're measuring different things. Let me verify this interpretation by checking if other objects follow the same pattern. no free slots) while still technically being in "idle" state because the animation hasn't started yet. Actually, looking more carefully at the bonsai: it shows "● full" but the bonsai state is "idle". Sol is walking → bonsai (reserved). So yes, this confirms: "full" means the slot is taken (reserved or occupied), even if the object animation state is "idle". This is actually correct behavior! The slot is reserved when a character decides to use it, before they arrive. This prevents race conditions where two characters both start walking to the same object. Now for Tea Set: it shows "● full" and "idle". Who reserved it? Looking at the characters... Maple is at bookshelf, Sol is walking to bonsai, Nyx has no target. But at 11:27 AM, Maple decided to interact with tea set. Maybe Maple already finished that interaction? No, Maple is still at bookshelf... Wait, there's a timing issue here. At 11:39 AM the bookshelf transitioned to "reading" state, which is after 11:27 AM when Maple decided to use the tea set. But Maple is currently at the bookshelf. So it seems like Maple must have used the tea set first between 11:27 and 11:39 AM, then moved to the bookshelf—though I'm not seeing an explicit tea set state event logged for that interaction. (no event shown, or event scrolled out) — the event log is probably limited to the most recent 10-12 entries, so there could be state transitions between 11:27 AM and now that I'm not seeing. Rather than keep analyzing the gaps, I should just run the simulation at 4x speed to generate more decision cycles, do a fresh reset to observe from the start, manually trigger contested resource situations with the use buttons, and watch for talk handshaking behavior.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #22
Excellent! Now I have a very rich event log. Let me analyze all the key findings: **Key Events Found:** 1. **TALK handshaking found!** - 10:25 AM: Nyx → talk → Maple "Good morning, Maple! How are you doing today?" [AI] - 10:31 AM: Nyx ↔ Maple [TALK] - talking state confirmed! 2. **REJECT found!** - 2:26 PM: Nyx → Maple rejected (interacting) [REJECT] - 2:26 PM: Nyx → talk → Maple "Hey Maple, how's the cooking going?" [AI] 3. **Tea Set (capacity 1) conflict handling:** - At 9:19 AM: Nyx → interact → tea set "It looks like Sol is already using the tea set. I'll wait until they're done." [AI] - The AI recognizes the tea set is occupied but doesn't immediately give up—instead it acknowledges the conflict and decides to wait. 4. **Kotatsu multi-capacity (2):** - 1:46 PM: Sol → interact → kotatsu [AI] - 1:52 PM: Nyx → interact → kotatsu "Ah, a spot of warmth and comfort would be lovely right now." [AI] - 3:14 PM: Maple → interact → kotatsu [AI] - 4:04 PM: Sol → interact → kotatsu "Time to cozy up under the kotatsu!" [AI] - The kotatsu displays "1/2 free," confirming it supports multiple characters simultaneously with capacity tracking. 5. **Object state machines:** - I'm seeing the state transitions for various objects: tea set cycles through brewing states, the fridge opens and closes, the bonsai gets tended, the easel progresses through painting stages, the stove cooks and finishes, and the bookshelf handles reading interactions. - The kotatsu and bed both follow simple idle-to-active-to-idle patterns. 6. **Display Names**: All events consistently use Maple, Sol, and Nyx rather than their alternate names. ✓ 7. **AI decision cycles**: The timestamps show decision intervals of roughly 23-33 minutes in game time, though Sol's gap appears larger at 64 minutes—but I need to account for the 4x speed multiplier where 30 real seconds equals 120 game seconds. I'm trying to figure out the exact time scaling. The AI next counter shows around 26-30 real seconds at 4x speed, which means game time advances much faster than real time. I need to work out whether the game clock is running at 4x the normal rate or if there's a different conversion happening. So the ~30 second cycle in the spec is measuring real time, not game time—at 1x speed that's 30 real seconds between AI decisions, which scales down to about 7.5 real seconds at 4x speed. Looking at the action log, I'm seeing idle and move actions from Nyx at various timestamps, which suggests the AI is making different types of decisions beyond just combat or interaction. Now I'm checking what's missing from the logs—I don't see any FALLBACK events recorded anywhere, which could mean either the fallback system is working silently when characters pick different objects, or it's not implemented yet. Looking back at the 8:00-8:01 AM window where all three characters tried to use the tea set, they all queued up the same interaction but I'd need to trace through the full log to see how that conflict was actually resolved. Then at 9:19 AM there's something revealing: Nyx's interaction with the tea set includes a quote saying "It looks like Sol is already using the tea set. I'll wait until they're done"—so the character is acknowledging they're waiting, but the logged action doesn't show how that decision was made or whether there's a FALLBACK event that should have been recorded. Looking at the sequence after that, the tea set becomes ready at 9:25 AM and Sol uses it at 9:34 AM, but I don't see Nyx actually returning to the tea set afterward. Instead, Nyx shows up at the bonsai at 10:02 AM, which suggests they either went somewhere else entirely or the log is missing that interaction. So Sol actually used the tea set between 9:34 and 9:38 AM, which means Nyx's decision to wait didn't pay off—Sol got there first. Now I need to verify the remaining tests: I've confirmed the slot capacity display and the talk handshaking mechanics, and I've seen the rejection when trying to talk to an interacting character, but I still need to complete the fourth test. Looking at what's left to verify, I need to check the fallback logic more carefully since I haven't seen clear FALLBACK events in the logs yet, and I should also confirm that characters display the purple talking badge, face each other during conversations, stay at objects for their full duration, and that the pathfinding avoids walls. I also want to test what happens when two characters want the same single-capacity object and whether the second one gets properly blocked. Let me take a screenshot to see if any characters are currently talking so I can verify the visual indicators. I'm noticing something odd in the current state—Sol is walking but their target shows as "—" even though they were heading to the kotatsu at 4:04 PM. The kotatsu shows 1/2 free with only Maple using it, so I need to understand whether Sol's target got cleared or if they're still trying to reach it. Let me take a screenshot to see what's actually happening right now, and then I'll search through the event log more carefully to check if there are any FALLBACK events that might explain unexpected behavior changes. Looking at the situations where characters tried to use the same object—like when both Maple and Sol went for the tea set at 8:01 AM, or when Nyx tried to use it later at 9:19 AM—these should logically trigger fallback behavior. But I'm not seeing FALLBACK events in the log. It's possible they're happening silently when a character arrives and discovers the object is full, rather than being logged as explicit events. Looking at that 43-minute gap between Nyx's tea set attempt and the bonsai action, I'd expect another AI decision cycle around 9:50 AM if they're running every 30 minutes, but there's nothing logged for Nyx there. This suggests FALLBACK events either aren't being recorded or aren't implemented at all, which would be a problem for the criterion about showing fallback reasons. Let me reset and observe the scenario more systematically, starting with checking the visual state of the slot indicators and how the kotatsu handles multiple characters.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #26
Great, now at 8:54 PM game time, I can see an interesting situation: **Current State:** - Stove: cooking, "● full" with orange pip, 10s remaining - TV: idle, "□□ 1/2 free" - one slot taken, one free - Fridge: idle, 1/1 free - Bookshelf: idle, 1/1 free - Tea Set: idle, "● full" - someone reserved it - Kotatsu: idle, 2/2 free - Desk: idle, 1/1 free - Bed: resting, "● full" with orange pip, 43s remaining - Easel: idle, 1/1 free - Bonsai: idle, 1/1 free **Characters:** - Maple: walking toward the TV (reserved), position (10,6), AI decision in 21s - Sol: using the bed, position (16,4), 43s remaining - Nyx: using the stove, position (3,2), 10s remaining **Event Log:** - 8:51 PM: Maple decided to watch TV for a bit - 8:41 PM: Nyx settled in, enjoying the cozy atmosphere - 8:36 PM: Bed transitioned to resting mode - 8:34 PM: Stove started cooking - 8:24 PM: Sol headed inside to find something to do - 8:19 PM: Maple moved toward the bed for the night - 8:06 PM: Nyx's action was cut off , but looking at what follows, Nyx went to the bookshelf to read - 8:04 PM: Bonsai stopped being tended and went idle - 8:02 PM: Bookshelf reading ended, returning to idle - 7:52 PM: Bonsai started being tended again - 7:52 PM: Sol got cozy under the kotatsu I'm noticing the capacity constraints working as expected—the TV shows one slot remaining since Maple claimed one, the bed is full with Sol resting, and the stove is occupied with cooking. The bonsai and bookshelf are cycling between active use and idle states as characters interact with them. TV slot pip shows partially filled (1/2 capacity used) I'm also noting the movement actions—Sol and Maple both have walk commands at different times, which are distinct from interaction attempts. There's also an idle decision from Nyx where the character chose to do nothing. So far I've confirmed the slot system displays capacity correctly, multi-capacity objects work as expected, and objects show full when occupied. The talk handshaking between Nyx and Maple went through—I saw the decision event, then the mutual talk event, though I should visually verify both characters displayed the purple talking badge. I also caught a rejection when Nyx tried to interact with Maple at 2:26 PM. I haven't spotted any explicit FALLBACK events in the log even though characters do seem to fall back to alternatives when their first choice is unavailable. Display names are all correct, and the core engine with AI decisions and object state machines is running smoothly. Now I'm going to reset and watch from the start again, focusing specifically on whether both characters show the purple badge when they're in a conversation.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #33
Interesting! After reset, only 2 events visible: - 8:07 AM: Nyx → interact → tea set [AI] - 8:09 AM: tea_set idle → brewing [auto] Wait - this is only showing 2 events after reset. But in the previous run, we also saw events from before the reset. Did the reset clear the event log? Looking at the log, it shows events from the new run starting at 8:07 AM. Yes, the reset cleared the log. But now there's a problem - the reset didn't fully reset! It says "8:12 AM" at the top. Now looking at the current state: the tea set is brewing with 13 seconds left, Maple's walking, Sol is interacting with the kotatsu, and Nyx is at the tea set. But there's a discrepancy—the kotatsu shows "2/2 free" even though Sol is actively using it, which should mean only 1 slot is available. Checking the timeline, at 4:04 PM the kotatsu correctly showed "1/2 free" when Sol started using it, so something's off with the current display. I'm wondering if the slot counter isn't being properly decremented when Sol interacts with multi-capacity objects, or if the reset cleared the slots without actually stopping Sol's interaction. The "2/2 free" notation suggests all slots are available, yet Sol claims to be using the kotatsu—this points to either a state synchronization issue after the reset or a bug in how multi-slot objects track active users. Looking at the event log for this new run starting at 8:12 AM, I don't see any kotatsu interaction event for Sol, which is odd given the "Hold: 41s" timer on their character card. Either the interaction carried over from the previous run as a reset bug, or the event scrolled out of view. The most likely explanation is that the initial state after reset has Sol pre-positioned at the kotatsu with an active hold timer, but the slot counter didn't update to reflect this occupancy. I should speed up the simulation and watch how this resolves as the run progresses, particularly to see if the talk handshaking works correctly from the beginning.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #37
Excellent! Now I have a very comprehensive event log. Let me analyze what I see: **REJECT found again!** - 10:03 AM: Nyx → talk → Sol "Good morning, Sol. How are you doing today?" [AI]- 10:04 AM: Nyx → Sol rejected (interacting) [REJECT]This is great evidence! Sol was interacting with something (the fridge, which shows "open → idle" at 10:08 AM and was "idle → open" at 9:58 AM), so Sol was opening the fridge from 9:58 AM onwards. When Nyx tried to talk to Sol at 10:03 AM, Sol was interacting with the fridge, so the REJECT was triggered. ✓ I'm looking back at the previous run to trace the talk handshaking between Nyx and Maple—I can see the decision event where Nyx initiated conversation at 10:25 AM, and the TALK event confirmed at 10:31 AM showing both entered talking state, though I need to verify the purple badge visually. Let me check the current state of the stove and other objects. The stove shows "idle" and "full" simultaneously, which mirrors that Tea Set bug I spotted earlier. Looking at the character states—Maple is walking, Sol is idle, and Nyx is idle—nobody is actually targeting the stove, yet it's marked as full, suggesting the slot is occupied even though no one should be using it. Tracing back through the event log, the stove transitioned from cooking to done at 10:03 AM, but there's no corresponding character interaction event for who actually used it between 8:33 AM and 10:03 AM. This looks like a stale slot reservation—the stove thinks it's occupied but the character who reserved it never logged an interaction. happened before the logged period. Looking at the earliest events visible: - 8:07 AM: Nyx → interact → tea set (first event) - 8:09 AM: tea_set idle → brewing - 8:25 AM: Maple → interact → bonsai - 8:25 AM: tea_set brewing → ready None of these are stove. But the stove started "idle → cooking" at 8:33 AM. This would be Sol or someone who decided to cook. Maybe: - Sol decided to cook at ~8:30 AM (but that event isn't in the log?), or - The log only shows the last ~40 events Actually wait, looking at this - I'm now thinking the kotatsu issue from before (showing "2/2 free" while Sol was using it) and this stove issue (showing "full" while nobody is using it) might both be related bugs. The kotatsu displayed as completely free when Sol was actively using it, which shouldn't happen. And the stove is showing as full despite no character cards indicating anyone's actually using it right now. nobody using it**: The stove shows "idle" state and "full" slot. But Maple is walking and Sol/Nyx are idle. None has a stove target. This is a stale slot reservation bug. Hmm, but let me also check: Maple is walking (target "—"), and "full" stove. Maybe Maple just decided to go to the stove but the target field hasn't updated yet? Actually, Maple has "AI next: 28s" which suggests Maple JUST made an AI decision recently. Maple is walking somewhere but the target shows "—". Looking at the events: - 10:17 AM: Sol → interact → TV "Maybe I'll watch some TV..." [AI] - tv on → idle at 10:13 AM And Maple's last visible decision in the log was... "9:53 AM: Maple → interact → fridge". Maple's AI next shows 28s, so there's a recent decision that hasn't appeared in the log yet. Given that the stove is marked "full" while showing "idle", it seems Maple reserved the stove slot but hasn't arrived to use it yet—the stove isn't cooking because Maple is still walking toward it. So "full" in the slot display means the slot is taken, whether actively in use or just reserved. Let me take a screenshot to see the current visual state and check for any slot pip visualization.

Given that the stove is marked \"full\" while showing \"idle\", it seems Maple reserved the stove slot but hasn't arrived to use it yet\u2014the stove isn't cooking because Maple is still walking toward it. So \"full\" in the slot display means the slot is taken, whether actively in use or just reserved.\n\nLet me take a screenshot to see the current visual state and check for any slot pip visualization.",

Now I can see: **Current State (11:32 AM):** - Stove: idle, "● full" - but nobody uses stove? Actually wait... - Let me look at the characters: - Maple: idle, position (2,2), target: —, AI next: 3s - Sol: walking, position (13,7), target: → bookshelf (reserved), AI next: 23s - Nyx: interacting, position (2,6), target: ◆ easel (using), Hold: 41s, AI next: 3s So: - Nyx is painting at the easel with 41 seconds remaining, Sol is heading to the bookshelf which is reserved, and Maple is idle and about to act in 3 seconds. The stove is showing as full but nobody's actually targeting it—that's definitely a stale reservation bug. Looking at the event log more carefully, I see Sol interacted with the bonsai at 11:28 AM, but Sol's current target shows the bookshelf as reserved. That's a contradiction—Sol decided on the bonsai but is now walking toward the bookshelf instead. The most likely explanation is that Sol completed the bonsai interaction quickly and then made a new decision to head to the bookshelf sometime after 11:28 AM, but that second decision event isn't showing up in the log yet. Or perhaps the bonsai interaction failed for some reason (occupied or blocked), forcing Sol to pivot to the bookshelf without a visible event record. I'm noticing the event log is actually ordered with the most recent events first, so Sol's last decision was the bonsai interaction at 11:28 AM. But Sol's current target is the bookshelf, which doesn't match—and bonsai is showing as free, so it wasn't a rejected action. I'm realizing I might be misreading the timestamps; the screenshot shows 11:32 AM game time, and the events span back from around 10:21 AM. Now I'm puzzled by the stove situation with Maple. The stove shows as full and reserved, but Maple is currently idle with no target, and Maple's next decision is in 3 seconds. This suggests either Maple just released the stove slot before the snapshot was taken, or Maple finished using it but the slot wasn't properly cleared. Let me trace through the event log to understand the stove's state transitions. The log shows the stove went from cooking to done at 10:03 AM, then immediately to idle at the same timestamp with an auto-transition. But I'm noticing a discrepancy—the stove is showing as full in the current snapshot even though no character has it targeted, which could be a display bug worth investigating for the QA test. The AI timer suggests Maple made a decision about 27 seconds ago, but the event log only shows interactions from 11:07 AM and earlier—nothing from the expected ~11:05 AM timeframe. There's a gap between what the timer indicates (a recent decision) and what's visible in the log, so Maple's most recent action should be appearing somewhere around 11:05 AM game time but isn't showing up. Actually, looking at Maple's action: "idle" with target "—". Maple chose to do nothing, which should still be logged like the other idle decisions I've seen before (Nyx at 12:21 PM and 1:21 PM both logged their idle choices). So if Maple went idle, it should appear in the log. The most likely explanation is that Maple's decision around 11:29-11:32 AM happened after the last visible event at 11:28 AM, but the log hadn't updated yet when I captured the snapshot. The same applies to Sol's bookshelf reservation—it was probably decided after the 11:28 AM bonsai event but hasn't appeared in the log. This tracks with their AI next counters: Maple at 3 seconds means she just decided something about 27 seconds ago, and Sol at 23 seconds means he decided something roughly 7 seconds ago, both mid-cycle rather than at the start of a new one. The stove showing "● full" while Maple has no target is the real puzzle here. It could be a display bug where the slot didn't clear properly, or possibly Maple just made a decision to use the stove that hasn't rendered yet, though her "idle" status makes that seem unlikely. Actually, thinking through the timeline: if Maple decided to use the stove 27 seconds ago, she should definitely be there by now since it's only 2 tiles away from her current position at (2,2). But she's showing "idle" with a 3-second AI timer, not "interacting" or "cooking". So either the stove interaction failed somehow, or there's a mismatch between what the display is showing and what's actually happening in the game state. Wait—I'm noticing the stove has an orange pip and shows "full" even though nobody's using it. That's suspicious. It looks like the stove might have a bug where after it finished cooking and transitioned from "done" back to "idle", the slot wasn't properly released. So it's stuck showing "full" permanently after that first use, even though it's not actually cooking anything. Let me check if this is the same pattern I saw with the Tea Set earlier—both showing "idle" + "full" at the same time, which shouldn't happen. The stove's been stuck in this state since going idle at 10:03 AM, consistently displaying "idle" + "full" across multiple screenshots. But I'm not seeing any character interaction event that triggered the initial cooking at 8:33 AM, which is strange—the stove started cooking without a visible decision from any character. Looking more carefully at the event log from the snapshot, I'm seeing events starting at 8:07 AM with Nyx using the tea set, then the tea set auto-transitioning to brewing at 8:09 AM, followed by Maple interacting with the bonsai at 8:25 AM. The log continues from there, but I still don't see the stove interaction event that would explain how cooking started at 8:33 AM. Looking at the timeline continuing through the morning, I'm tracking more state transitions—the fridge cycling open and closed, characters moving between activities like the bookshelf and TV, and then hitting a rejection event when Nyx tries to interact with Sol who's already occupied, before successfully initiating a conversation instead. AM: fridge open → idle [auto] 10:13 AM: tv on → idle [auto] 10:17 AM: Sol → TV [AI] (more recent events below this) So between the 8:07 AM (first visible event) and 8:33 AM (stove starts cooking), there must have been a character decision to use the stove that SHOULD be visible but isn't. Wait... 8:07 AM is Nyx's first decision. Maybe Sol or Maple decided to use the stove earlier but those events are NOT visible? But they should be between 8:07 AM and 8:33 AM in the timeline... OH WAIT. I think I misread the snapshot. The events are actually in reverse chronological order in the data—the oldest event is at the bottom (uid 5_223-5_226: "8:07 AM: Nyx → tea set [AI]") and the newest is at the top (uid 5_107-5_115: "10:17 AM: Sol → TV [AI]"). So the visible log spans from 8:07 AM to 10:17 AM, but there's still no character decision for the stove between 8:07 AM and when it starts cooking at 8:33 AM. Either someone triggered it before the visible events begin, or it auto-started without a recorded character action. Actually, looking more carefully at the gap: between 8:07 AM (Nyx tea set) and 8:25 AM (Maple bonsai), there's nothing. Then between 8:25 AM and 8:33 AM (stove starts), there's still nothing. So whoever decided to cook didn't have their decision logged—this looks like a logging bug where the character's "interact → stove" event wasn't captured. I'm now thinking the stove interaction event for whichever character initiated cooking might have failed to log, or it got lost somehow in the event stream. ), Hold: 41s, AI next: 2s - Nyx: interacting with tea set, position (5,4), Hold: 13s, AI next: 20s So Sol was already locked into the kotatsu interaction at the start of this run with 41 seconds remaining on the hold timer, meaning that decision happened before 8:07 AM and never got logged. The same pattern might explain the missing stove event around 8:30 AM—someone made that decision earlier but the event record didn't capture it, or it got overwritten before the first visible log entry. Looking at the timestamps more carefully, both Sol and Nyx likely made their initial interaction decisions at the same moment (8:07 AM), but only Nyx's tea set event appears in the log. This could be a simultaneous event collision where the system only recorded the last update, losing Sol's kotatsu decision from that same cycle. The stove interaction probably suffered the same fate—another simultaneous event that vanished from the record. Now I'm seeing a clearer pattern in the actual bugs. The kotatsu displayed "2/2 free" immediately after the reset while Sol was actively using it, which should have shown "1/2 free"—that's definitely a slot tracking bug triggered by the reset. And there's a second issue where objects aren't properly releasing their slots when transitioning from active states back to idle, like the stove staying marked "full" even when nobody's using it. Let me trace through whether Maple is actually still occupying that stove slot or if it's just a display error. position (2,2) with no target. The STOVE is at tile (3,3). Looking at the current screenshot at 11:32 AM: Actually, I wonder if MAPLE is the one who has the stove reserved. Looking at Maple's position (2,2) and stove (3,3) - they're adjacent. Maple is "idle" not "walking". But earlier I noted "AI next: 3s" - Maple is about to make a decision. If Maple made a decision 27s ago to use the stove, and the stove is only 1-2 tiles away, Maple would have arrived quickly, reserved the slot, started interacting, and FINISHED the interaction (if it was very brief) in 27 seconds. Then the stove would go from idle → cooking → done → idle? No, the stove's timer is longer than 27 seconds. What if Maple decided to cook, walked to the stove, and is currently interacting but the display shows idle? Or maybe Maple finished the interaction but the slot wasn't released properly. Either way, I'm spending too much time on this one issue. Let me focus on the critical items for the QA report: the talking state purple badge, any FALLBACK-type events in the logs, the kotatsu "2/2 free" bug after reset, and this stale slot reservation on the stove.     "thinking": "Now I can see:\n\n**Current State (11:32 AM):**\n- Stove: idle, \"\u25cf full\" - but nobody uses stove? Actually wait...\n- Let me look at the characters:  - Maple: idle, position (2,2), target: \u2014, AI next: 3s\n  - Sol: walking, position (13,7), target: \u2192 bookshelf (reserved), AI next: 23s\n  - Nyx: interacting, position (2,6), target: \u25c6 easel (using), Hold: 41s, AI next: 3s\n\nSo:\n\n\n- Nyx is painting at the easel with 41 seconds remaining, Sol is heading to the bookshelf which is reserved, and Maple is idle and about to act in 3 seconds. The stove is showing as full but nobody's actually targeting it\u2014that's definitely a stale reservation bug.\n\nLooking at the event log more carefully, I see Sol interacted with the bonsai at 11:28 AM, but Sol's current target shows the bookshelf as reserved. That's a contradiction\u2014Sol decided on the bonsai but is now walking toward the bookshelf instead.\n\nThe most likely explanation is that Sol completed the bonsai interaction quickly and then made a new decision to head to the bookshelf sometime after 11:28 AM, but that second decision event isn't showing up in the log yet. Or perhaps the bonsai interaction failed for some reason (occupied or blocked), forcing Sol to pivot to the bookshelf without a visible event record. I'm noticing the event log is actually ordered with the most recent events first, so Sol's last decision was the bonsai interaction at 11:28 AM. But Sol's current target is the bookshelf, which doesn't match\u2014and bonsai is showing as free, so it wasn't a rejected action. I'm realizing I might be misreading the timestamps; the screenshot shows 11:32 AM game time, and the events span back from around 10:21 AM.\n\nNow I'm puzzled by the stove situation with Maple. The stove shows as full and reserved, but Maple is currently idle with no target, and Maple's next decision is in 3 seconds. This suggests either Maple just released the stove slot before the snapshot was taken, or Maple finished using it but the slot wasn't properly cleared.\n\nLet me trace through the event log to understand the stove's state transitions. The log shows the stove went from cooking to done at 10:03 AM, then immediately to idle at the same timestamp with an auto-transition. But I'm noticing a discrepancy\u2014the stove is showing as full in the current snapshot even though no character has it targeted, which could be a display bug worth investigating for the QA test. The AI timer suggests Maple made a decision about 27 seconds ago, but the event log only shows interactions from 11:07 AM and earlier\u2014nothing from the expected ~11:05 AM timeframe. There's a gap between what the timer indicates (a recent decision) and what's visible in the log, so Maple's most recent action should be appearing somewhere around 11:05 AM game time but isn't showing up.\n\nActually, looking at Maple's action: \"idle\" with target \"\u2014\". Maple chose to do nothing, which should still be logged like the other idle decisions I've seen before (Nyx at 12:21 PM and 1:21 PM both logged their idle choices). So if Maple went idle, it should appear in the log.\n\nThe most likely explanation is that Maple's decision around 11:29-11:32 AM happened after the last visible event at 11:28 AM, but the log hadn't updated yet when I captured the snapshot. The same applies to Sol's bookshelf reservation\u2014it was probably decided after the 11:28 AM bonsai event but hasn't appeared in the log. This tracks with their AI next counters: Maple at 3 seconds means she just decided something about 27 seconds ago, and Sol at 23 seconds means he decided something roughly 7 seconds ago, both mid-cycle rather than at the start of a new one.\n\nThe stove showing \"\u25cf full\" while Maple has no target is the real puzzle here. It could be a display bug where the slot didn't clear properly, or possibly Maple just made a decision to use the stove that hasn't rendered yet, though her \"idle\" status makes that seem unlikely.\n\nActually, thinking through the timeline: if Maple decided to use the stove 27 seconds ago, she should definitely be there by now since it's only 2 tiles away from her current position at (2,2). But she's showing \"idle\" with a 3-second AI timer, not \"interacting\" or \"cooking\". So either the stove interaction failed somehow, or there's a mismatch between what the display is showing and what's actually happening in the game state.\n\nWait\u2014I'm noticing the stove has an orange pip and shows \"full\" even though nobody's using it. That's suspicious. It looks like the stove might have a bug where after it finished cooking and transitioned from \"done\" back to \"idle\", the slot wasn't properly released. So it's stuck showing \"full\" permanently after that first use, even though it's not actually cooking anything.\n\nLet me check if this is the same pattern I saw with the Tea Set earlier\u2014both showing \"idle\" + \"full\" at the same time, which shouldn't happen.\n\nThe stove's been stuck in this state since going idle at 10:03 AM, consistently displaying \"idle\" + \"full\" across multiple screenshots. But I'm not seeing any character interaction event that triggered the initial cooking at 8:33 AM, which is strange\u2014the stove started cooking without a visible decision from any character.\n\nLooking more carefully at the event log from the snapshot, I'm seeing events starting at 8:07 AM with Nyx using the tea set, then the tea set auto-transitioning to brewing at 8:09 AM, followed by Maple interacting with the bonsai at 8:25 AM. The log continues from there, but I still don't see the stove interaction event that would explain how cooking started at 8:33 AM. Looking at the timeline continuing through the morning, I'm tracking more state transitions\u2014the fridge cycling open and closed, characters moving between activities like the bookshelf and TV, and then hitting a rejection event when Nyx tries to interact with Sol who's already occupied, before successfully initiating a conversation instead. AM: fridge open \u2192 idle [auto]\n10:13 AM: tv on \u2192 idle [auto]\n10:17 AM: Sol \u2192 TV [AI]\n(more recent events below this)\n\nSo between the 8:07 AM (first visible event) and 8:33 AM (stove starts cooking), there must have been a character decision to use the stove that SHOULD be visible but isn't. Wait... 8:07 AM is Nyx's first decision. Maybe Sol or Maple decided to use the stove earlier but those events are NOT visible? But they should be between 8:07 AM and 8:33 AM in the timeline...\n\nOH WAIT. I think I misread the snapshot. The events are actually in reverse chronological order in the data\u2014the oldest event is at the bottom (uid 5_223-5_226: \"8:07 AM: Nyx \u2192 tea set [AI]\") and the newest is at the top (uid 5_107-5_115: \"10:17 AM: Sol \u2192 TV [AI]\"). So the visible log spans from 8:07 AM to 10:17 AM, but there's still no character decision for the stove between 8:07 AM and when it starts cooking at 8:33 AM. Either someone triggered it before the visible events begin, or it auto-started without a recorded character action.\n\nActually, looking more carefully at the gap: between 8:07 AM (Nyx tea set) and 8:25 AM (Maple bonsai), there's nothing. Then between 8:25 AM and 8:33 AM (stove starts), there's still nothing. So whoever decided to cook didn't have their decision logged\u2014this looks like a logging bug where the character's \"interact \u2192 stove\" event wasn't captured.\n\nI'm now thinking the stove interaction event for whichever character initiated cooking might have failed to log, or it got lost somehow in the event stream. ), Hold: 41s, AI next: 2s\n- Nyx: interacting with tea set, position (5,4), Hold: 13s, AI next: 20s\n\nSo Sol was already locked into the kotatsu interaction at the start of this run with 41 seconds remaining on the hold timer, meaning that decision happened before 8:07 AM and never got logged. The same pattern might explain the missing stove event around 8:30 AM\u2014someone made that decision earlier but the event record didn't capture it, or it got overwritten before the first visible log entry.\n\nLooking at the timestamps more carefully, both Sol and Nyx likely made their initial interaction decisions at the same moment (8:07 AM), but only Nyx's tea set event appears in the log. This could be a simultaneous event collision where the system only recorded the last update, losing Sol's kotatsu decision from that same cycle. The stove interaction probably suffered the same fate\u2014another simultaneous event that vanished from the record.\n\nNow I'm seeing a clearer pattern in the actual bugs. The kotatsu displayed \"2/2 free\" immediately after the reset while Sol was actively using it, which should have shown \"1/2 free\"\u2014that's definitely a slot tracking bug triggered by the reset. And there's a second issue where objects aren't properly releasing their slots when transitioning from active states back to idle, like the stove staying marked \"full\" even when nobody's using it. Let me trace through whether Maple is actually still occupying that stove slot or if it's just a display error. position (2,2) with no target. The STOVE is at tile (3,3). Looking at the current screenshot at 11:32 AM:\n\nActually, I wonder if MAPLE is the one who has the stove reserved. Looking at Maple's position (2,2) and stove (3,3) - they're adjacent. Maple is \"idle\" not \"walking\". But earlier I noted \"AI next: 3s\" - Maple is about to make a decision. \n\nIf Maple made a decision 27s ago to use the stove, and the stove is only 1-2 tiles away, Maple would have arrived quickly, reserved the slot, started interacting, and FINISHED the interaction (if it was very brief) in 27 seconds. Then the stove would go from idle \u2192 cooking \u2192 done \u2192 idle? No, the stove's timer is longer than 27 seconds.\n\nWhat if Maple decided to cook, walked to the stove, and is currently interacting but the display shows idle? Or maybe Maple finished the interaction but the slot wasn't released properly. Either way, I'm spending too much time on this one issue. Let me focus on the critical items for the QA report: the talking state purple badge, any FALLBACK-type events in the logs, the kotatsu \"2/2 free\" bug after reset, and this stale slot reservation on the stove. I'll wait for a few more decision cycles to gather more data.\n\nI should scroll through the page to see if there's additional information or if the slot pips display differently, and I'll also check the memory file to understand the project context better.",
    "signature": ASSISTANTMESSAGE #53
Excellent! Now I have an extremely long event log. Let me analyze the key items: **KOTATSU Multi-Capacity:** At 3:36 PM: Nyx → interact → kotatsu "Ahh, the kotatsu looks so cozy right now. I think I'll join Sol and relax under it for a bit." [AI] - Note: Nyx says "I'll join Sol" - this suggests the AI is AWARE that Sol is at the kotatsu and choosing to JOIN them! This confirms multi-capacity behavior.At 2:49 PM: Sol → interact → kotatsu [AI] At 2:52 PM: kotatsu idle → cozy [auto] At 3:36 PM: Nyx → interact → kotatsu [AI] (joining Sol) At 3:37 PM: kotatsu cozy → idle [auto] So the kotatsu stayed cozy for about 45 minutes while Sol was there, then Nyx joined at 3:36 PM and just a minute later it went idle at 3:37 PM - which is odd timing. Either Sol left right as Nyx arrived, or something else triggered the state change. This confirms the kotatsu has a capacity of 2 and the AI is definitely aware of other characters using the same object. I've been scanning through this massive event log and I'm noticing something significant - there are zero FALLBACK events anywhere. The log contains character decisions, object state changes, REJECT events, TALK events, but nothing labeled as FALLBACK. Either these events aren't being generated at all, or they're happening silently without a log entry. Now I'm looking at some conflicts with the tea set capacity. Let me trace through what happened with that object. → tea set [AI] - 1:44 PM: Nyx → tea set [AI] Both decided to use the tea set at nearly the same time! tea_set was idle at this point (1:28 PM: brewing → ready, then ready → idle at 1:49 PM? No wait...) Let me trace more carefully: - 1:13 PM: tea_set idle → brewing (first use at 1:13 PM) - 1:28 PM: tea_set brewing → ready (ready at 1:28 PM) - 1:42 PM: Maple → interact → tea set [AI] (decided at 1:42 PM) - 1:44 PM: Nyx → interact → tea set [AI] (decided at 1:44 PM) - 1:49 PM: tea_set ready → idle [auto] (went idle at 1:49 PM) So the tea set was ready when Maple decided to use it at 1:42 PM, then Nyx decided at 1:44 PM, and it went idle at 1:49 PM. Both of them decided to use it almost simultaneously, but since the tea set has capacity 1, one of them should have been blocked. Looking at what happened next—the easel started painting and the fridge opened, both at 1:49 PM—I need to figure out which interaction actually succeeded. Continuing through the timeline, Maple interacted with the tea set again at 2:12 PM, it started brewing at 2:15 PM, and was ready by 2:30 PM. Then Nyx came back at 2:36 PM and the tea set went idle at 2:38 PM. So both of them got turns, but I'm still trying to understand the exact sequence of what happened at 1:42-1:49 PM when they both tried to use it. :42 PM Maple decided tea set and at 1:44 PM Nyx decided tea set (capacity 1). The tea set went idle at 1:49 PM. Then at 2:12 PM Maple decided AGAIN (and tea set started brewing at 2:15 PM). And at 2:36 PM Nyx decided AGAIN. This pattern suggests: 1. At 1:42 PM, Maple reserved the tea set slot 2. At 1:44 PM, Nyx tried to use tea set - was it blocked or did Nyx also reserve it? 3. At 1:49 PM, tea_set ready → idle (someone used the ready state) 4. But who? And what happened to the other person? The fact that Maple decided tea set again at 2:12 PM (only 30 minutes later) suggests either Maple used it at 1:42 PM and wanted it again, or was blocked initially and retried. Similarly, Nyx's second attempt at 2:36 PM (52 minutes after the first) indicates either successful use or being blocked by Maple. Since both tried within minutes of each other with capacity 1, only one could have succeeded initially. tea_set went "brewing → ready" at 1:28 PM (before either decided). Then both decided to use the "ready" tea set. The tea set went "ready → idle" at 1:49 PM. This is ONE transition, suggesting only ONE person used it. The fact that BOTH Maple and Nyx came back to the tea set ~30 minutes later suggests NEITHER of them successfully used it! Or maybe ONE did and the other found out it was gone. But there's NO FALLBACK event in the log between 1:42 PM and 2:12 PM for Maple. That gap of 30 minutes is one AI cycle. After being blocked from tea set, Maple's next AI decision would be at ~2:12 PM (30 minutes later in game time), which is exactly when Maple decided to try tea set again. For Nyx, there's a 52-minute gap between the 1:44 PM tea set attempt and the next visible decision at 2:36 PM—that's roughly two AI cycles, but I'm only seeing one decision logged. Looking at the timeline, Nyx went idle at 1:16 PM, then tried the tea set at 1:44 PM, and doesn't appear again until 2:36 PM. That missing intermediate decision around 2:14 PM suggests either Nyx was stuck in a blocked state or made a decision that didn't generate a visible log entry. PM and went idle (no action). But the "idle" decision would be logged like "1:16 PM: Nyx → idle [AI]". We don't see that for Nyx between 1:44 PM and 2:36 PM. OR: Nyx did use the tea set at 1:44 PM (tea_set ready → idle at 1:49 PM = Nyx used the ready tea set), then Nyx's next AI decision was at ~2:14 PM (30 min later) and decided something else (maybe moved/idle), and then at 2:36 PM decided tea set again. But we don't see Nyx's ~2:14 PM decision in the log. I think the event log has a maximum capacity or there are events that aren't being logged (like simple movements or idle decisions not shown). Looking at the sheer volume of events spanning from 10:21 AM to 4:28 PM—that's over 6 hours of game time with multiple characters making decisions roughly every 30 minutes—I'm seeing what appears to be a comprehensive log, though some routine actions might be filtered out. Let me stop speculating about missing data and focus on what I can actually verify: the slot capacity displays are working correctly, showing full/partial states accurately, and multi-capacity objects like the kotatsu properly reflect when they're partially occupied. I'm spotting three key issues: the stove is showing "full" even when idle and no one's using it, suggesting a slot wasn't properly released after use. The kotatsu capacity display is wrong after reset—it showed 2/2 free while Sol was actively interacting with it. And I'm not seeing any FALLBACK events in the logs at all, even though characters are clearly moving between objects when blocked, which means the fallback mechanism isn't being logged properly. There are also some missing AI decision events—like the stove transitioning to "cooking" without a visible interact event—and I still need to verify several visual behaviors: the purple talking badge, characters facing each other during conversations, talk timers counting down, characters staying at objects for their full duration, proper A* pathfinding without wall clipping, and whether a second character trying to use a capacity-1 object gets an explicit "blocked" message. Let me grab a screenshot to check the current state—the TV should show "on" with "1/2 free" if one character is using it, and I want to see if that earlier Nyx interaction with the kotatsu is still visible.

Then Nyx came back at 2:36 PM and the tea set went idle at 2:38 PM. So both of them got turns, but I'm still trying to understand the exact sequence of what happened at 1:42-1:49 PM when they both tried to use it. :42 PM Maple decided tea set and at 1:44 PM Nyx decided tea set (capacity 1). The tea set went idle at 1:49 PM. Then at 2:12 PM Maple decided AGAIN (and tea set started brewing at 2:15 PM). And at 2:36 PM Nyx decided AGAIN.\n\nThis pattern suggests:\n1. At 1:42 PM, Maple reserved the tea set slot\n2. At 1:44 PM, Nyx tried to use tea set - was it blocked or did Nyx also reserve it?\n3. At 1:49 PM, tea_set ready \u2192 idle (someone used the ready state)\n4. But who? And what happened to the other person?\n\nThe fact that Maple decided tea set again at 2:12 PM (only 30 minutes later) suggests either Maple used it at 1:42 PM and wanted it again, or was blocked initially and retried. Similarly, Nyx's second attempt at 2:36 PM (52 minutes after the first) indicates either successful use or being blocked by Maple. Since both tried within minutes of each other with capacity 1, only one could have succeeded initially. tea_set went \"brewing \u2192 ready\" at 1:28 PM (before either decided). Then both decided to use the \"ready\" tea set. The tea set went \"ready \u2192 idle\" at 1:49 PM. This is ONE transition, suggesting only ONE person used it.\n\nThe fact that BOTH Maple and Nyx came back to the tea set ~30 minutes later suggests NEITHER of them successfully used it! Or maybe ONE did and the other found out it was gone.\n\nBut there's NO FALLBACK event in the log between 1:42 PM and 2:12 PM for Maple. That gap of 30 minutes is one AI cycle. After being blocked from tea set, Maple's next AI decision would be at ~2:12 PM (30 minutes later in game time), which is exactly when Maple decided to try tea set again.\n\nFor Nyx, there's a 52-minute gap between the 1:44 PM tea set attempt and the next visible decision at 2:36 PM\u2014that's roughly two AI cycles, but I'm only seeing one decision logged. Looking at the timeline, Nyx went idle at 1:16 PM, then tried the tea set at 1:44 PM, and doesn't appear again until 2:36 PM. That missing intermediate decision around 2:14 PM suggests either Nyx was stuck in a blocked state or made a decision that didn't generate a visible log entry. PM and went idle (no action). But the \"idle\" decision would be logged like \"1:16 PM: Nyx \u2192 idle [AI]\". We don't see that for Nyx between 1:44 PM and 2:36 PM.\n\nOR: Nyx did use the tea set at 1:44 PM (tea_set ready \u2192 idle at 1:49 PM = Nyx used the ready tea set), then Nyx's next AI decision was at ~2:14 PM (30 min later) and decided something else (maybe moved/idle), and then at 2:36 PM decided tea set again.\n\nBut we don't see Nyx's ~2:14 PM decision in the log.\n\nI think the event log has a maximum capacity or there are events that aren't being logged (like simple movements or idle decisions not shown). Looking at the sheer volume of events spanning from 10:21 AM to 4:28 PM\u2014that's over 6 hours of game time with multiple characters making decisions roughly every 30 minutes\u2014I'm seeing what appears to be a comprehensive log, though some routine actions might be filtered out.\n\nLet me stop speculating about missing data and focus on what I can actually verify: the slot capacity displays are working correctly, showing full/partial states accurately, and multi-capacity objects like the kotatsu properly reflect when they're partially occupied. I'm spotting three key issues: the stove is showing \"full\" even when idle and no one's using it, suggesting a slot wasn't properly released after use. The kotatsu capacity display is wrong after reset\u2014it showed 2/2 free while Sol was actively interacting with it. And I'm not seeing any FALLBACK events in the logs at all, even though characters are clearly moving between objects when blocked, which means the fallback mechanism isn't being logged properly.\n\nThere are also some missing AI decision events\u2014like the stove transitioning to \"cooking\" without a visible interact event\u2014and I still need to verify several visual behaviors: the purple talking badge, characters facing each other during conversations, talk timers counting down, characters staying at objects for their full duration, proper A* pathfinding without wall clipping, and whether a second character trying to use a capacity-1 object gets an explicit \"blocked\" message.\n\nLet me grab a screenshot to check the current state\u2014the TV should show \"on\" with \"1/2 free\" if one character is using it, and I want to see if that earlier Nyx interaction with the kotatsu is still visible.",

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #57
Excellent! Now I can see some important things: **Current State (8:37 PM):** - Stove: idle, "● full" (STILL showing full - confirming the stale slot bug)- Fridge: idle, 1/1 free - Tea Set: idle, "□ 1/1 free" (correct - free!)- Kotatsu: idle, 2/2 free- TV: idle, 2/2 free - **Bookshelf: reading, "● full", 15s remaining** - orange pip ✓ - **Desk: working, "● full", 22s remaining** - orange pip ✓ (new state! "working")- Bed: idle, "● full" (another stale slot? Or Sol reserved it?) - **Easel: painting, "● full", 10s remaining** - orange pip ✓ And now I'm seeing all three characters actively engaged: Maple's at the desk working for 22 more seconds, Sol's using the bookshelf for 15 seconds, and Nyx is painting at the easel with 10 seconds left. They're all simultaneously occupied with their respective activities. Looking at the object states, the desk is showing "working" which confirms these objects have their own state machines. The stove and bed are still showing that odd "● full" state while sitting idle, which seems like a persistent bug—nobody's even using the bed but it's marked as full. The TV's the only one showing proper free slots at 2/2. The event log shows the desk transitioning from idle to working around 8:23 PM, and there's a partial entry from 8:11 PM about the bookshelf. Scanning through the rest of the event log, I can see the bookshelf went from idle to reading state, Sol interacted with it, Nyx went to the tea set, the easel started painting, and the TV turned off. The tea set cycled through several states—brewing, ready, idle—with Maple and Nyx both interacting with it at different times. There's a lot of activity happening across these objects and characters. Looking at the timeline more carefully, Maple initiated the tea set interaction at 7:59 PM when it was ready, and it transitioned to idle a minute later at 8:00 PM—suggesting Maple actually used it. Then Nyx decided on the tea set at 8:02 PM, but the logs don't show any subsequent brewing cycle, and Nyx is currently at the easel, so it seems Nyx never actually followed through with that decision. Nyx's AI decision at 8:02 PM for the tea set contradicts the current state where Nyx is at the easel—either Nyx decided on the easel first (triggering the 8:01 PM start), then reconsidered tea set at 8:02 PM, or the easel decision log entry is missing entirely and Nyx switched targets after the tea set decision was logged. 8:02 PM was "interact → tea set", which scheduled Nyx to go to the tea set. But at 8:01 PM, easel started painting (from a PREVIOUS Nyx easel decision from before 8:02 PM). And now Nyx is at the easel with Hold: 10s. The 8:02 PM tea set decision might have been OVERRIDDEN by the fact that Nyx was already committed to the easel. I think what's happening is the AI fires roughly every 30 real seconds regardless of whether Nyx is already interacting with something. At 8:02 PM it decided to switch to the tea set, but since Nyx was already locked into the easel interaction with a Hold timer, that new decision got queued or ignored. The character stays committed to finishing the current interaction before the next decision can take effect. The AI decision firing at 0s could override Nyx's planned tea set interaction once the easel hold finishes in 10 seconds, which would explain the unpredictable behavior. Looking at the bed issue now—it shows "● full" but is actually idle with nobody using it, and the event log shows it went idle at 8:00 PM, yet 37 minutes later it's still displaying as full instead of showing 1/1 free slots. This looks like the same stale state bug I'm seeing with the stove. But wait, other objects like the fridge, tea set, bonsai, easel, and bookshelf all correctly show "1/1 free" when idle, so the slot release mechanism works sometimes. The pattern seems to be that stove and bed specifically aren't freeing their slots after completing their cycles—tea set goes idle → brewing → ready → idle and releases correctly, while stove and bed get stuck showing full even after returning to idle. I'm thinking the slot release might be tied to when the character actually leaves the object rather than when the object state changes. For tea set and bookshelf, the character leaves as the object transitions to its final idle state, but with stove and bed, maybe the character leaves before that final transition completes, or there's a missing signal that tells the slot it's safe to release. Looking at the stove timeline more carefully—it goes idle → cooking → done → idle across those timestamps—the "full" status persisting suggests the slot was never actually released even after the stove returned to idle. The character should have left sometime between when cooking finished and when the stove cycled back to idle, so there's likely a bug in how that departure is being tracked or communicated to the slot system. The bed shows the same pattern: it transitioned to idle at 8:00 PM but still displays "full" later at 8:37 PM, which shouldn't happen unless a character is somehow still occupying it despite the state change. Looking at the character cards, nobody is actually at the bed—Maple's at the desk, Sol's at the bookshelf, and Nyx's at the easel. So this confirms the bug: both the stove and bed aren't properly releasing their slots after use transitions to idle. I'm also noticing the stove started without any visible AI interaction event, which is another anomaly worth tracking. The pip visualization correctly shows which objects are actively in use, but there's a bug where the pips stay filled even after an object finishes its cycle instead of resetting to hollow. I still need to visually confirm a few things: whether the purple talking badge appears on character cards during conversations, if characters face each other when talking, and whether a talk timer counts down on the cards. For pathfinding, I should watch characters navigate around obstacles. On the capacity issue, I've seen both characters decide to use the same single-capacity object in the logs, but the second character's blocking behavior isn't explicitly marked as a fallback—they just move on to something else. Since TALK events appear regularly in the logs, I'll run the simulation at 4x speed and capture screenshots when I spot a TALK event to catch the visual state. PM. Scanning through the entire event log from 10:21 AM to 8:37 PM, I'm not finding any TALK or REJECT events after that 10:04 AM rejection—which means there haven't been any interaction attempts in the past 10+ hours of game time. Comparing this to earlier snapshots where I saw TALK events followed by REJECTs around 2:26 PM and 10:03 AM, it's striking that this current run shows only rejections with no successful talks at all. Talk events seem pretty infrequent, so I'm going to keep the simulation running at 4x speed and watch for when one actually occurs. Rather than just passively waiting, I think I should use the evaluate_script tool to directly query the game state and pull information about talk events programmatically instead of relying on manual log inspection.
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": "Excellent! Now I can see some important things:\n\n**Current State (8:37 PM):**\n- Stove: idle, \"\u25cf full\" (STILL showing full - confirming the stale slot bug)- Fridge: idle, 1/1 free\n- Tea Set: idle, \"\u25a1 1/1 free\" (correct - free!)- Kotatsu: idle, 2/2 free- TV: idle, 2/2 free\n- **Bookshelf: reading, \"\u25cf full\", 15s remaining** - orange pip \u2713\n- **Desk: working, \"\u25cf full\", 22s remaining** - orange pip \u2713 (new state! \"working\")- Bed: idle, \"\u25cf full\" (another stale slot? Or Sol reserved it?)\n\n\n- **Easel: painting, \"\u25cf full\", 10s remaining** - orange pip \u2713\n\nAnd now I'm seeing all three characters actively engaged: Maple's at the desk working for 22 more seconds, Sol's using the bookshelf for 15 seconds, and Nyx is painting at the easel with 10 seconds left. They're all simultaneously occupied with their respective activities.\n\nLooking at the object states, the desk is showing \"working\" which confirms these objects have their own state machines. The stove and bed are still showing that odd \"\u25cf full\" state while sitting idle, which seems like a persistent bug\u2014nobody's even using the bed but it's marked as full. The TV's the only one showing proper free slots at 2/2. The event log shows the desk transitioning from idle to working around 8:23 PM, and there's a partial entry from 8:11 PM about the bookshelf.\n\nScanning through the rest of the event log, I can see the bookshelf went from idle to reading state, Sol interacted with it, Nyx went to the tea set, the easel started painting, and the TV turned off. The tea set cycled through several states\u2014brewing, ready, idle\u2014with Maple and Nyx both interacting with it at different times. There's a lot of activity happening across these objects and characters. Looking at the timeline more carefully, Maple initiated the tea set interaction at 7:59 PM when it was ready, and it transitioned to idle a minute later at 8:00 PM\u2014suggesting Maple actually used it. Then Nyx decided on the tea set at 8:02 PM, but the logs don't show any subsequent brewing cycle, and Nyx is currently at the easel, so it seems Nyx never actually followed through with that decision. Nyx's AI decision at 8:02 PM for the tea set contradicts the current state where Nyx is at the easel\u2014either Nyx decided on the easel first (triggering the 8:01 PM start), then reconsidered tea set at 8:02 PM, or the easel decision log entry is missing entirely and Nyx switched targets after the tea set decision was logged. 8:02 PM was \"interact \u2192 tea set\", which scheduled Nyx to go to the tea set. But at 8:01 PM, easel started painting (from a PREVIOUS Nyx easel decision from before 8:02 PM). And now Nyx is at the easel with Hold: 10s. The 8:02 PM tea set decision might have been OVERRIDDEN by the fact that Nyx was already committed to the easel.\n\nI think what's happening is the AI fires roughly every 30 real seconds regardless of whether Nyx is already interacting with something. At 8:02 PM it decided to switch to the tea set, but since Nyx was already locked into the easel interaction with a Hold timer, that new decision got queued or ignored. The character stays committed to finishing the current interaction before the next decision can take effect. The AI decision firing at 0s could override Nyx's planned tea set interaction once the easel hold finishes in 10 seconds, which would explain the unpredictable behavior. Looking at the bed issue now\u2014it shows \"\u25cf full\" but is actually idle with nobody using it, and the event log shows it went idle at 8:00 PM, yet 37 minutes later it's still displaying as full instead of showing 1/1 free slots. This looks like the same stale state bug I'm seeing with the stove.\n\nBut wait, other objects like the fridge, tea set, bonsai, easel, and bookshelf all correctly show \"1/1 free\" when idle, so the slot release mechanism works sometimes. The pattern seems to be that stove and bed specifically aren't freeing their slots after completing their cycles\u2014tea set goes idle \u2192 brewing \u2192 ready \u2192 idle and releases correctly, while stove and bed get stuck showing full even after returning to idle.\n\nI'm thinking the slot release might be tied to when the character actually leaves the object rather than when the object state changes. For tea set and bookshelf, the character leaves as the object transitions to its final idle state, but with stove and bed, maybe the character leaves before that final transition completes, or there's a missing signal that tells the slot it's safe to release.\n\nLooking at the stove timeline more carefully\u2014it goes idle \u2192 cooking \u2192 done \u2192 idle across those timestamps\u2014the \"full\" status persisting suggests the slot was never actually released even after the stove returned to idle. The character should have left sometime between when cooking finished and when the stove cycled back to idle, so there's likely a bug in how that departure is being tracked or communicated to the slot system.\n\nThe bed shows the same pattern: it transitioned to idle at 8:00 PM but still displays \"full\" later at 8:37 PM, which shouldn't happen unless a character is somehow still occupying it despite the state change.\n\nLooking at the character cards, nobody is actually at the bed\u2014Maple's at the desk, Sol's at the bookshelf, and Nyx's at the easel. So this confirms the bug: both the stove and bed aren't properly releasing their slots after use transitions to idle.\n\nI'm also noticing the stove started without any visible AI interaction event, which is another anomaly worth tracking. The pip visualization correctly shows which objects are actively in use, but there's a bug where the pips stay filled even after an object finishes its cycle instead of resetting to hollow. I still need to visually confirm a few things: whether the purple talking badge appears on character cards during conversations, if characters face each other when talking, and whether a talk timer counts down on the cards.\n\nFor pathfinding, I should watch characters navigate around obstacles. On the capacity issue, I've seen both characters decide to use the same single-capacity object in the logs, but the second character's blocking behavior isn't explicitly marked as a fallback\u2014they just move on to something else. Since TALK events appear regularly in the logs, I'll run the simulation at 4x speed and capture screenshots when I spot a TALK event to catch the visual state. PM. Scanning through the entire event log from 10:21 AM to 8:37 PM, I'm not finding any TALK or REJECT events after that 10:04 AM rejection\u2014which means there haven't been any interaction attempts in the past 10+ hours of game time. Comparing this to earlier snapshots where I saw TALK events followed by REJECTs around 2:26 PM and 10:03 AM, it's striking that this current run shows only rejections with no successful talks at all.\n\nTalk events seem pretty infrequent, so I'm going to keep the simulation running at 4x speed and watch for when one actually occurs. Rather than just passively waiting, I think I should use the evaluate_script tool to directly query the game state and pull information about talk events programmatically instead of relying on manual log inspection.",

{
  "function": "() => {\n  // Try to get the game state\n  const gameState = window.__gameState || window.gameState || window.sim;\n  if (gameState) return JSON.stringify(Object.keys(gameState));\n  \n  // Try to find Phaser game\n  const game = window.game || (window.Phaser && window.Phaser.game);\n  if (game) return \"Found Phaser game: \" + typeof game;\n  \n  return \"No game state found. Keys: \" + Object.keys(window).filter(k => !['performance', 'location', 'document', 'window', 'navigator'].includes(k)).join(', ');\n}"
}

      "function": "() => {\n  // Try to get the game state\n  const gameState = window.__gameState || window.gameState || window.sim;\n  if (gameState) return JSON.stringify(Object.keys(gameState));\n  \n  // Try to find Phaser game\n  const game = window.game || (window.Phaser && window.Phaser.game);\n  if (game) return \"Found Phaser game: \" + typeof game;\n  \n  return \"No game state found. Keys: \" + Object.keys(window).filter(k => !['performance', 'location', 'document', 'window', 'navigator'].includes(k)).join(', ');\n}"
    },

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #62
mcp__chrome_devtools__evaluate_script TOOL CALL 
{
  "function": "() => {\n  // Look for Phaser canvas and game\n  const canvas = document.querySelector('canvas');\n  if (canvas && canvas.__phaser) return \"Found phaser on canvas\";\n  \n  // Try to find the game via React root or other frameworks\n  const gameDiv = document.getElementById('game') || document.querySelector('[data-game]');\n  if (gameDiv) return \"Found game div: \" + gameDiv.id;\n  \n  // Check for sim engine\n  const simKeys = Object.keys(window).filter(k => k.match(/sim|engine|game|world|char|event/i));\n  return \"Sim-related keys: \" + simKeys.join(', ') + \"\\n_dbInteract: \" + typeof window._dbInteract;\n}"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",

    "name": "mcp__chrome_devtools__evaluate_script",
    "input": {
      "function": "() => {\n  // Look for Phaser canvas and game\n  const canvas = document.querySelector('canvas');\n  if (canvas && canvas.__phaser) return \"Found phaser on canvas\";\n  \n  // Try to find the game via React root or other frameworks\n  const gameDiv = document.getElementById('game') || document.querySelector('[data-game]');\n  if (gameDiv) return \"Found game div: \" + gameDiv.id;\n  \n  // Check for sim engine\n  const simKeys = Object.keys(window).filter(k => k.match(/sim|engine|game|world|char|event/i));\n  return \"Sim-related keys: \" + simKeys.join(', ') + \"\\n_dbInteract: \" + typeof window._dbInteract;\n}"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

# evaluate_script response
Script ran on page and returned:
```json
"function(objectId, action) {
      const obj = simEngine.objectEngine.getById(objectId);
      if (!obj) return;
      const from = obj.state;
      const ev = simEngine.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);
      if (ev) {
        pushLog(objectId, from, ev.state, action);
        prevStateMap.set(objectId, ev.state);
        updateObjectCard(obj);
      }
    }"
```

        "type": "text",
        "text": "# evaluate_script response\nScript ran on page and returned:\n```json\n\"function(objectId, action) {\\n      const obj = simEngine.objectEngine.getById(objectId);\\n      if (!obj) return;\\n      const from = obj.state;\\n      const ev = simEngine.objectEngine.interact(objectId, action);\\n      if (ev) {\\n        pushLog(objectId, from, ev.state, action);\\n        prevStateMap.set(objectId, ev.state);\\n        updateObjectCard(obj);\\n      }\\n    }\"\n```"
      }
    ]
  }
}

}
